headline: Synthetic supply discipline scenario
summary: A producer group signals tighter quota compliance which would reduce expected near-term barrels and support crude prices.

headline: Synthetic refinery maintenance scenario
summary: Seasonal refinery maintenance lowers crude runs and can soften prompt demand for feedstock.

headline: Synthetic inventory draw scenario
summary: A larger-than-expected stockpile draw points to tighter physical balances and can support WTI and Brent.

headline: Synthetic dollar strength scenario
summary: A stronger U.S. dollar makes dollar-priced crude more expensive for non-U.S. buyers and can pressure prices.

headline: Synthetic tanker route delay scenario
summary: Longer tanker routes increase freight time and delivery uncertainty which can add a risk premium to seaborne crude.

headline: Synthetic regional tension scenario
summary: Elevated regional risk raises concern about potential supply interruptions and supports a higher risk premium.

headline: Synthetic demand recovery scenario
summary: Improving travel and industrial activity would increase expected crude consumption and support prices.

headline: Synthetic crude build scenario
summary: A weekly inventory build suggests supply is outpacing near-term demand and may weigh on prices.

headline: Synthetic rate-cut delay scenario
summary: Higher-for-longer interest rate expectations can weaken risk appetite and dampen demand expectations.

headline: Synthetic port congestion scenario
summary: Port congestion can delay supply arrivals but also disrupt refinery scheduling so the price signal is mixed.

headline: Synthetic non-OPEC output growth scenario
summary: Rising non-OPEC supply adds incremental barrels and can offset producer group cuts.

headline: Synthetic sanctions risk scenario
summary: New sanctions risk could restrict trade flows and create uncertainty around available export supply.

headline: Synthetic mobility slowdown scenario
summary: Softer mobility indicators suggest weaker fuel demand and a less supportive consumption outlook.

headline: Synthetic strategic reserve purchase scenario
summary: Government reserve buying would add incremental demand and may support the front of the curve.

headline: Synthetic inflation surprise scenario
summary: Sticky inflation may pressure financial conditions while also increasing interest in real assets.

headline: Synthetic canal transit delay scenario
summary: Reduced canal capacity forces some cargoes onto longer routes and can tighten regional availability.

headline: Synthetic ceasefire discussion scenario
summary: A lower perceived conflict risk can reduce the geopolitical premium embedded in crude prices.

headline: Synthetic quota rollover scenario
summary: Extending output restraint keeps expected supply growth limited and supports market balances.

headline: Synthetic product demand softness scenario
summary: Weak gasoline and diesel demand would reduce refinery crude demand and pressure prices.

headline: Synthetic surprise stock draw scenario
summary: A surprise draw indicates tighter-than-expected balances and may lead analysts to revise demand higher.

headline: Synthetic dollar pullback scenario
summary: A weaker U.S. dollar can improve affordability for international buyers and support commodity prices.

headline: Synthetic tanker insurance cost scenario
summary: Higher insurance costs for key routes can raise delivered crude costs and support benchmark prices.

headline: Synthetic diplomatic escalation scenario
summary: Escalating diplomatic tension increases uncertainty around supply continuity and trade flows.

headline: Synthetic producer compliance concern scenario
summary: Questions about quota compliance weaken confidence in supply discipline and may pressure prices.

headline: Synthetic industrial demand upgrade scenario
summary: Improved manufacturing indicators point to stronger distillate and petrochemical feedstock demand.

headline: Synthetic storage capacity concern scenario
summary: Rising storage levels suggest looser physical supply and may weigh on nearby crude prices.

headline: Synthetic central bank risk-on scenario
summary: Improved risk appetite and easier financial conditions can support cyclical commodities including crude.

headline: Synthetic export terminal outage scenario
summary: A temporary terminal outage can reduce export availability and support prices until flows normalize.

headline: Synthetic pipeline restart scenario
summary: The restart of a constrained logistics asset can improve supply availability and reduce local price support.

headline: Synthetic conflict de-escalation scenario
summary: Lower conflict probability can remove part of the risk premium that had supported prices.

headline: Synthetic voluntary cut extension scenario
summary: A voluntary production cut extension lowers expected supply and supports a tighter balance narrative.

headline: Synthetic airline fuel demand scenario
summary: Stronger airline activity raises jet fuel consumption expectations and can support crude demand.

headline: Synthetic refined product build scenario
summary: Product stock builds can imply weaker end-user demand and reduce refinery appetite for crude.

headline: Synthetic dollar rangebound scenario
summary: A rangebound dollar gives no strong directional signal for crude and leaves supply-demand factors in focus.

headline: Synthetic freight normalization scenario
summary: Normalizing freight conditions reduce logistics risk premiums and can soften delivered crude costs.

headline: Synthetic hurricane preparation scenario
summary: Weather preparation can temporarily affect offshore operations and refinery scheduling with uncertain net impact.

headline: Synthetic border disruption risk scenario
summary: Border disruption risk can threaten cross-border flows and support prices through a supply risk premium.

headline: Synthetic spare capacity comment scenario
summary: Discussion of spare capacity has an unclear near-term effect without a confirmed change in output policy.

headline: Synthetic emerging market demand concern scenario
summary: Weaker emerging market growth assumptions can reduce expected oil demand and pressure forward balances.

headline: Synthetic crude stock unchanged scenario
summary: Flat inventories do not provide a strong directional signal and require confirmation from demand indicators.

headline: Synthetic mixed macro data scenario
summary: Conflicting macro indicators make the demand outlook less certain and can keep crude rangebound.

headline: Synthetic shipping lane reopening scenario
summary: A reopened shipping lane lowers route risk and can reduce the logistics premium in seaborne crude.
